question: Has your company measured and recorded direct greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, e.g., fuel combustion, company vehicles) and indirect energy emissions (Scope 2, e.g., purchased electricity)?
hint: Scope 1 refers to emissions directly generated by the company's own operations; Scope 2 refers to indirect emissions from purchased electricity, steam, heating, or cooling

options.label: Yes, fully measured and recorded in accordance with the GHG Protocol
Partially measured, or not yet using a standard methodology
No, have not started measuring greenhouse gas emissions

question: Is your company aware of and planning to measure value chain greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 3, e.g., supply chain, logistics, product use phase)?
hint: Scope 3 covers upstream procurement, employee commuting, product transportation, downstream use, and other indirect emission categories

options.label: Yes, Scope 3 emissions have been measured and disclosed by GHG Protocol category
Partially, understand the Scope 3 concept and are building data collection capabilities
No, not yet aware of or planning Scope 3 measurement

question: Has your company identified climate-related risks that may affect your business (e.g., extreme weather, policy changes, shifts in market demand)?
hint: Climate risks are classified as physical risks (typhoons, flooding, etc.) and transition risks (carbon taxes, technology obsolescence, changing market preferences, etc.)

options.label: Yes, systematically identified both physical and transition risks, and assessed short-, medium-, and long-term impacts
Partially, initial identification conducted but not systematic, or risk types and time horizons not differentiated
No, have not undertaken climate risk identification

question: Has your company conducted climate scenario analysis (e.g., simulating business impacts under different warming scenarios) or a climate resilience assessment?
hint: Scenario analysis typically includes assessing financial and operational impacts under 1.5C, 2C, and 4C warming pathways

options.label: Yes, completed quantitative or qualitative scenario analysis and assessed strategic resilience
Partially, conducted only a preliminary qualitative analysis
No, have not conducted any scenario analysis

question: Has your company set explicit carbon reduction targets (including base year, target year, and reduction magnitude)?
hint: Science-Based Targets (SBTi) or net-zero commitments are internationally recognized approaches for setting emission reduction targets

options.label: Yes, established quantitative reduction targets with KPIs, timelines, and a baseline year
Partially, have qualitative targets or some elements are missing
No, have not set any climate-related targets

question: Does your company regularly monitor and disclose energy consumption, water usage, and waste generation?
hint: This includes electricity consumption, water consumption, hazardous and non-hazardous waste volumes, and related intensity metrics

options.label: Yes, comprehensively disclosed data on energy, water, and waste
Partially, disclosed only some resource usage data
No, do not monitor or disclose resource usage

question: Do your exports to the EU involve steel, aluminium, cement, fertilisers, electricity, or hydrogen? If so, are you aware of the reporting obligations under the EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)?
hint: CBAM requires importers to report carbon emission data and purchase carbon certificates; it takes full effect from 2026

options.label: Yes, understand CBAM obligations and have established a carbon emission reporting process
Partially, aware of CBAM but have not yet established a compliance process
No, not aware of CBAM, or do not export the relevant products to the EU

question: Does your company factor carbon costs into business decisions (e.g., internal carbon pricing, carbon taxes, emissions trading scheme impacts)?
hint: Internal carbon pricing is a company-set price on carbon emissions, used for investment decisions and risk assessment

options.label: Yes, apply internal carbon pricing or assess external carbon costs in decision-making
Partially, understand the concept of carbon costs but have not systematically incorporated them into decisions
No, have not considered carbon cost factors

question: Does your company collect and disclose total workforce headcount and turnover rate, broken down by gender, age group, and region?
hint: Workforce data includes full-time, part-time, and temporary worker classifications, as well as turnover rates across dimensions

options.label: Yes, fully disclosed workforce data across multiple dimensions
Partially, have basic workforce statistics but incomplete dimension coverage
No, have not conducted disaggregated workforce data collection

question: Does your company track and record workplace safety incidents (injuries, fatalities, lost workdays)?
hint: Key indicators include injury rate, number of fatalities, and lost workdays due to work-related injuries

options.label: Yes, fully recorded and disclosed workplace safety incident data for the past three years
Partially, have records but data is incomplete or not publicly disclosed
No, do not systematically record safety incident data

question: Does your company provide regular training for employees, and track training coverage rates and average training hours per employee?
hint: Training includes professional skills, workplace safety, and ESG-related programmes

options.label: Yes, have a systematic training plan, with training data tracked by gender and employee category
Partially, provide training but do not systematically record data
No, do not have a formal employee training programme

question: Does your company have a system to manage environmental and social risks in the supply chain (e.g., supplier code of conduct, green procurement standards)?
hint: This includes supplier screening, environmental compliance requirements, and labour standards audits

options.label: Yes, have a comprehensive supply chain ESG management system and understand supplier numbers and distribution
Partially, have basic supplier management but it does not cover ESG risks
No, do not have a supply chain ESG management system

question: Does your company's board of directors or senior management explicitly oversee climate and ESG-related matters?
hint: This includes the board's ESG oversight statement and the allocation of management responsibilities for climate issues

options.label: Yes, the board has clearly defined ESG oversight responsibilities with specific management assignments
Partially, have basic oversight but responsibilities are unclear or not formalised
No, the board and management are not involved in ESG matters

question: Has your company established a process for identifying, assessing, and monitoring climate-related risks?
hint: This includes risk management policies, periodic risk assessments, and integration with the overall enterprise risk management framework

options.label: Yes, established a complete process integrated into the enterprise-wide risk management framework
Partially, have some processes in place but not systematised or integrated
No, do not have a climate risk management process

question: Has your company published or is it preparing to publish an annual ESG or sustainability report?
hint: A sustainability report typically covers environmental performance, social responsibility, and corporate governance information

options.label: Yes, regularly publish an ESG report and make it publicly available through official channels
Partially, have plans but have not yet published, or the report is incomplete
No, have not published and have no plans to do so

question: Has your company adopted or is it planning to adopt an international sustainability reporting framework (e.g., GRI, IFRS S1/S2, TCFD)?
hint: Major frameworks include GRI (Global Reporting Initiative), IFRS S1/S2 (International Sustainability Standards), and TCFD (Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures)

options.label: Yes, selected a framework and prepare reports in accordance with its requirements
Partially, familiar with the frameworks but have not formally adopted one
No, not aware of or not considering adopting a reporting framework

question: Has your company's ESG or sustainability data been externally verified or is it planned to be verified by an independent third party (e.g., limited assurance by an auditor)?
hint: External verification enhances report credibility; certain jurisdictions have mandatory verification requirements for emission data

options.label: Yes, engaged a third-party verification body operating to international standards
Partially, aware of the requirements and planning to arrange verification, but have not yet done so
No, not aware of external verification requirements

question: Does your company have an anti-corruption system in place (including preventive measures, reporting channels, and employee training)?
hint: An anti-corruption system covers commercial bribery, conflicts of interest, whistleblower protection, and related policies

options.label: Yes, have a comprehensive anti-corruption system with preventive measures, reporting channels, and training
Partially, have basic policies but they are not comprehensive
No, do not have a formal anti-corruption system
